paper_title: Micropaleontological and Morphometric Analysis of Planktonic Foraminifera in the Central Mediterranean Sea

methods.description: This method involves the collection and analysis of planktonic foraminiferal assemblages from core-top samples to study their distribution and abundance patterns. The analysis helps in understanding the ecological and biogeographic implications of these species in the Mediterranean Sea.

steps.description: The planktonic foraminiferal assemblages for the studied core-tops were picked from 10 cm3 of wet sediment after washing through a 125 µm mesh sieve and cleaning using the HyPerCal protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: Process sediment samples through a 125 µm mesh sieve and clean using HyPerCal protocol

steps.description: The dry residues (~3 g) were split using an Otto micro-splitter into aliquots of at least 300 planktonic foraminiferal specimens, which were identified to the species level.
steps.searchable_summary: Split dry residues into aliquots and identify foraminiferal specimens to species level

steps.description: Raw data were transformed into percentages of the total absolute abundance, and relative percentage abundance curves were plotted versus latitude.
steps.searchable_summary: Transform raw data into percentage abundance and plot curves versus latitude

methods.description: This method involves the morphometric analysis of foraminiferal specimens to measure their size and shape parameters. The analysis is used to understand the size variability and distribution patterns of foraminiferal species.

steps.description: The picked specimens were transferred with a brush on a chapman micro-slide, where each species positioned in separate cells.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer specimens to chapman micro-slide and position in separate cells

steps.description: The fixed specimens were photographed in transmitted light under a 50-fold magnification by a modular Leica M165 C stereo microscope equipped with a Leica IC90 E color camera.
steps.searchable_summary: Photograph specimens under 50-fold magnification using Leica microscope

steps.description: The derived images were parsed into objects by thresholding and their shape and size parameters were automatically extracted using ImageJ software.
steps.searchable_summary: Parse images and extract shape and size parameters using ImageJ

steps.description: The Equivalent Circular Diameter (ECD) was measured as an aspect of the size of the species analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure Equivalent Circular Diameter (ECD) of species

methods.method_name: Sediment Sampling Strategy and Site Selection
methods.description: This method outlines the strategy for selecting sediment sampling sites and collecting core-top samples to study the distribution and abundance of planktonic foraminiferal fauna in the central Mediterranean Sea.

steps.description: The samples were collected by multicores during various expeditions and cover a latitudinal NW-SE transect from the north Adriatic to the south Ionian Sea.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment samples using multicores along NW-SE transect

steps.description: SST and SSS data were derived from the Emodnet database, while density data obtained from the NOAA dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch SST and SSS data from Emodnet and density data from NOAA

steps.description: The Chl-a concentrations at each core location were used as satellite data retrieved from OBPG MODIS-Aqua Monthly Global 4 km database.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve Chl-a concentrations from OBPG MODIS-Aqua database
